title: Sanji and Zeff’s Starvation Flashback
description: On pages 9–12, Sanji’s past with Zeff is revealed through their starvation on a rock, explaining the debt that binds him to the Baratie.
scene: The flashback strips away the noise of battle and focuses on hunger, sacrifice, and the dream of creating a restaurant where no one is denied food.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I'm sure it'll come in just a few more days...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Of course! I'm an adult, so it's only natural that I eat more.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: And he still has all that food left!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: He has... that much food still left...!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: It's all that Geezer's fault to begin with anyways!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I'll kill him and steal his food...

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: What did you come here for, you little eggplant.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I've come here to take your food!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Kill me if you can! I'm going to die anyways if I go without food any longer.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: How dare you take so much to eat all by youRse...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: didn't I say no contact until one of us sees a ship?

dialogue.speaker: Pearl
dialogue.text: It's all jewels, pearls, gold, and whatnot...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: to be filthy rich... yet can't get any food to eat...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Where's all the food!? Just how did you survive until now!?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Didn't you have that huge bag of food!?

summary: This part gives emotional meaning to Sanji’s choices: Zeff’s sacrifice explains why feeding the hungry is sacred to him.
